message: beads: disable auto-start daemon by default

files.patch:
@@ -30,6 +30,17 @@ bd update <issue-id> --status done
 bd sync
 ```
 
+## Daemon (Optional)
+
+Beads has an optional background daemon (`bd daemon`) that auto-syncs issues with git.
+
+In this repo, the sync branch is configured as `main`. Running the daemon while you are on a different branch can cause `.beads/issues.jsonl` to be rewritten from `main`, leaving your working tree dirty and blocking `git pull --rebase`.
+
+Recommended workflow:
+- Prefer manual sync: `bd sync` (default).
+- If you use the daemon, run it only while you are on `main`, or run it in local-only mode: `bd daemon --start --local`.
+- If `.beads/issues.jsonl` is changing unexpectedly, check/stop the daemon: `bd daemon --status` / `bd daemon --stop`.
+
 ### Working with Issues
 
 Issues in Beads are:

@@ -32,7 +32,7 @@
 # db: ""
 
 # Auto-start daemon if not running (can also use BEADS_AUTO_START_DAEMON)
-# auto-start-daemon: true
+auto-start-daemon: false
 
 # Debounce interval for auto-flush (can also use BEADS_FLUSH_DEBOUNCE)
 # flush-debounce: "5s"

@@ -420,6 +420,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-xv9e","title":"TASK: Implement cheatsheet terminal renderer","description":"# TASK: Implement cheatsheet terminal renderer ## Context Render the parsed aliases as a beautiful, categorized cheatsheet in the terminal. ## Implementation ### Function ```bash cheatsheet_render() { local filter="${1:-}" # Get parsed aliases local aliases aliases=$(cheatsheet_parse_aliases) # Group by category # Render with gum/plain formatting # Apply filter if provided } ``` ### Features - Categorized sections with headers - Two-column layout (alias → description) - Color coding by category - Search/filter by pattern - Gum-enhanced with fallback ### Search Mode ```bash acfs cheatsheet git # Filter to git category acfs cheatsheet "push" # Search for 'push' in name/desc ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Categorized output - [ ] Filterable by category or search term - [ ] Readable on 80-column terminal - [ ] Gum-enhanced with plain fallback - [ ] Shows description/expansion for each alias","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:58:32.632274-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T15:12:03.093174-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T15:12:03.093174-05:00","close_reason":"Complete: cheatsheet.sh with dynamic alias parsing, terminal/JSON output, filtering, integrated into acfs CLI","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-xv9e","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-thvw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:58:55.542635-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-xvvd","title":"Add 'Type yes' prompt reassurance and explanation","description":"# Task: Add 'Type yes' Prompt Reassurance ## Parent Epic [EPIC] First SSH Connection Experience (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-q7r3) ## Description The SSH host authenticity warning is TERRIFYING to beginners. It uses security language ("fingerprint", "Are you sure?") that sounds like a virus warning. Users need reassurance that this is normal. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/ssh-connect/page.tsx Add OutputPreview showing the scary prompt with explanation: \`\`\`tsx

You'll see a security message — that's normal!

The authenticity of host '45.123.67.89' can't be established.

ED25519 key fingerprint is SHA256:xYz123...

Are you sure you want to continue connecting (yes/no)?

 This is normal! Your computer is saying: "I've never talked to this server before. Do you trust it?" Since you just created this VPS, the answer is yes.

Type yes (the full word, not just "y") and press Enter.
\`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Shows the actual prompt users will see - [ ] Explains why it appears in simple terms - [ ] Reassures that it's expected and safe - [ ] Emphasizes typing "yes" not just "y" - [ ] Uses calming visual style (info/green, not warning/red) ## UI/UX Notes - This MUST appear before the SSH command, not buried in SimplerGuide - Use a Shield icon to reinforce safety - The explanation should feel like a friendly heads-up, not a warning - Consider adding: "You'll only see this once per VPS"","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:41:14.730547-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:02:13.053967-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:02:13.053967-05:00","close_reason":"Already covered: ssh-connect/page.tsx Step 5 explains 'Type yes' prompt in detail"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-y0d","title":"Docs reference missing VM test script","description":"AGENTS.md and README.md reference tests/vm/test_install_ubuntu.sh, but only tests/vm/.gitkeep exists. This confuses contributors and breaks documented commands.","acceptance_criteria":"Either: - Add tests/vm/test_install_ubuntu.sh that actually runs a meaningful installer test, OR - Update AGENTS.md and README.md to remove/replace the reference.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T14:48:12.189137-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T14:52:41.74062-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T14:52:41.74062-05:00","close_reason":"Added missing tests/vm/test_install_ubuntu.sh (Docker-based integration test) and updated README/AGENTS installer testing docs."} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-y0g9","title":"Bug: Beads daemon clobbers .beads/issues.jsonl on feature branches","description":"Observed bd daemon auto-import pulling sync branch (configured as main) and overwriting .beads/issues.jsonl in the working tree even when current branch HEAD contains newer committed beads. This causes persistent dirty working trees and blocks git pull/rebase.\n\nRepro: start bd daemon, create/commit a beads change on a non-main local branch that is ahead of origin/main, then any JSONL write triggers daemon to pull sync branch main and rewrite .beads/issues.jsonl back to origin/main version.\n\nFix: disable auto-start-daemon by default for this repo and document safe usage (run daemon only on main, or use --local).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-25T10:06:18.006234-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T10:07:44.877861-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T10:07:44.877861-05:00","close_reason":"Disable auto-start daemon in repo config and document safe daemon usage"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad","title":"EPIC: Website Wizard MVP","description":"# Epic: Website Wizard MVP ## Overview Build the step-by-step wizard website that guides beginners from "I have a laptop" to "paste the curl|bash command". ## Background & Reasoning The wizard is the user's first touchpoint with ACFS. It must: - Feel premium and trustworthy - Never overwhelm with jargon - Make every step feel achievable - Handle both Mac and Windows users - Guide through VPS provider selection ## Tech Stack - Next.js 16 App Router - Bun runtime - Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui - Vercel hosting + Cloudflare caching ## Key Design Principles 1. **Single action per screen** - One button or one command 2. **Copy button on every command** - Reduce friction 3. **"I did it" checkbox** - Gates "Next" button 4. **Collapsible troubleshooting** - Don't clutter but be ready ## Success Criteria - [ ] All 10 wizard steps implemented - [ ] OS-aware command rendering - [ ] Mobile responsive - [ ] Lighthouse score > 90 - [ ] Works without JavaScript (progressive enhancement) ## Relationship to Project Goals The wizard is the "front door" to ACFS. A confused or frustrated user at this stage will never reach the VPS installer. Excellence here directly enables the 10-minute promise.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:20:46.959703-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T11:25:29.453083-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T11:25:29.453083-05:00","close_reason":"All 15 wizard steps implemented and verified. Epic complete.","labels":["epic","website"]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.1","title":"Initialize Next.js 16 App Router project","description":"# Task: Initialize Next.js 16 App Router Project ## Description Set up the Next.js 16 project in apps/web/ with: - App Router structure - TypeScript configuration - Tailwind CSS - shadcn/ui components ## Technical Details ```bash cd apps/web bunx create-next-app@latest . --typescript --tailwind --eslint --app --src-dir bunx shadcn-ui@latest init ``` ## Configuration - Use Bun as package manager - Configure for Vercel deployment - Set up path aliases (@/components, @/lib) - Configure next.config.js for optimal settings ## Reasoning Next.js 16 with App Router provides: - Modern React Server Components - Automatic code splitting - Optimal performance defaults - Easy Vercel deployment ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Next.js 16 project created - [ ] TypeScript strict mode enabled - [ ] Tailwind CSS working - [ ] shadcn/ui initialized - [ ] bun run dev works - [ ] bun run build succeeds","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:22:18.88745-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:27:32.977854-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:27:32.977854-05:00","close_reason":"Next.js 16 project initialized with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, shadcn/ui, and turbopack.
